mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation signals eroding real returns and prompts expectations of future monetary policy tightening by the central bank. This increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest CPIAUCSL value of 278.88 reflects multi-decade high inflation.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 6-month change in CPIAUCSL and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03 in magnitude over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a key threshold, it signals a very tight labor market. This condition fuels wage-push inflation, forcing the central bank to tighten monetary policy more aggressively than anticipated, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest UNRATE at 4.2% is below many contemporary estimates of the non-accelerating inflation rate of unemployment (NAIRU).
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when the trigger condition (UNRATE < 4.5%) is met.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of persistently strong consumer demand, proxied by retail sales growth, and an already tightening monetary policy, proxied by a rising Fed Funds rate, signals an 'overheating' economy. Markets price in a higher probability of a policy error and a future 'hard landing', leading to negative returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS > 1% and 3-month change in FEDFUNDS > 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales remain robust while Fed officials are signaling an accelerated tightening timeline.
failure_condition: A regression model shows the interaction term between RSAFS changes and FEDFUNDS changes is not a statistically significant (p > 0.05) negative predictor of 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production indicates expanding economic output and robust corporate earnings growth. This fundamental strength translates into positive equity market returns, particularly as the economy emerges from a downturn.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month growth rate of INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative during the 24 months following an NBER-declared recession trough.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls is a primary coincident indicator of economic health. The market reacts positively and quickly to this confirmation of a robust labor market and implied consumer spending power, driving short-term positive returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The monthly jobs report is one of the most closely watched economic releases by market participants.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a top-decile monthly change in PAYEMS is not statistically greater than the unconditional average 5-day return.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in housing starts is a leading indicator of a slowdown in a critical, interest-rate sensitive sector of the economy. This signals declining consumer confidence and investment, portending broader economic weakness and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average crosses below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: A strategy that shorts the S&P 500 for 60 trading days after the trigger condition is met fails to generate a positive information ratio over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of slowing real economic growth and accelerating inflation creates a stagflationary environment. This regime is exceptionally hostile to equities, as it simultaneously compresses corporate profit margins and forces the central bank to tighten into weakness.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year GDPC1 growth < 2% AND Year-over-year CPIAUCSL growth > 4%
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflation was characterized by poor equity performance.
failure_condition: During periods where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: The rate of change of real GDP is the most comprehensive measure of economic activity. Positive acceleration in real GDP growth directly implies a stronger environment for aggregate corporate earnings, supporting higher equity prices over the medium term.
failure_condition: The rolling 5-year correlation between quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically greater than zero.
